overview: Simic Scapeshift leverages landfall synergies and massive mana acceleration to overwhelm opponents before they can stabilize. By cycling lands into the graveyard and replaying them with Scapeshift, the deck generates explosive board states and outscales most Pioneer strategies. Success relies on carefully sequencing land drops to maximize triggers while maintaining enough resources to cast high-impact threats.
win_conditions: Casting high-mana-cost threats that close the game
Flooding the board with mana-producing lands to outpace opponent's removal

keep_criteria: Hands with two or more lands and a ramp spell
Scapeshift paired with early landfall triggers
Spelunking to fix mana and set up the graveyard
mulligan_criteria: Zero lands or only high-cost cards
Hands lacking early interaction or acceleration

early_game: Play lands consistently to build a base
Use Spelunking to fetch specific land types and sacrifice lands to the graveyard
Deploy early landfall creatures to apply pressure
mid_game: Cast Scapeshift to replay lands from the graveyard
Chain landfall triggers to deploy threats and generate value
Manage life total carefully when using life-drain ramp
late_game: Flood the board with additional lands to produce massive mana
Cast win-condition threats that scale with land count
Use excess mana to cast multiple spells or activate abilities

key_cards.name: Scapeshift
key_cards.notes: Cast when you have lands in the graveyard to trigger landfall and accelerate mana production.

key_cards.name: Lumra, Bellow of the Woods
key_cards.notes: Enters with a landfall trigger and untaps two lands, enabling faster Scapeshift casts.

key_cards.name: Spelunking
key_cards.notes: Search for needed lands and put others into the graveyard to fuel Scapeshift, but watch your life total.

sequencing_tips: Prioritize putting lands into the graveyard before casting Scapeshift
Trigger landfall abilities in an order that maximizes board impact
Save Spelunking for when you need specific land types or graveyard fuel

matchups.advice: Ramp quickly to stabilize and use landfall triggers to outvalue their early pressure.
Race their hand disruption by flooding the board with lands and casting threats they cannot answer.
Outpace their curve by accelerating mana and deploying multiple threats per turn.
